tasks.title: Refactor quest objective completion detection into unified QuestCompletionEngine
tasks.description: Create server/services/quest-completion-engine.ts as a centralized engine that evaluates all 19 canonical objective types against real game state. Currently quest completion checks are scattered across BabylonGame.ts, QuestObjectManager.ts, and route handlers. (1) Define a QuestCompletionEngine class with evaluateObjective(objective, gameState) → { completed: boolean, progress: number, evidence: string }. (2) Implement evaluators for each canonical type in shared/quest-objective-types.ts: visit_location checks player position vs locationRadius, talk_to_npc checks conversation log, use_vocabulary checks vocabulary tracker, collect_item checks inventory, etc. (3) Add a checkAllObjectives(quest, gameState) method that returns per-objective status plus overall quest completion (all objectives completed = quest completed). (4) Wire this into both server-side route handlers and export a lightweight client-compatible version for real-time UI updates. (5) Include unit tests in server/__tests__/quest-completion-engine.test.ts covering each objective type.

tasks.title: Implement quest checklist UI with per-objective completion indicators
tasks.description: Enhance client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts quest log panel (toggled via J key) to show a clear checklist for each active quest. (1) Each quest objective renders as a checkbox line item with description text. (2) Completed objectives show a green checkmark and strikethrough text. (3) In-progress objectives show a progress bar (e.g., '3/5 vocabulary words used'). (4) Incomplete objectives show an empty checkbox. (5) When all objectives are checked off, the quest title gets a gold border and 'COMPLETE - Return to [NPC]' label. (6) Add subtle animation when an objective transitions from incomplete to complete (checkmark slide-in). (7) Use Babylon.js GUI StackPanel with per-objective rows. (8) Quest checklist updates in real-time as the QuestCompletionEngine emits progress events via GameEventBus.

tasks.title: Add conversation analysis pipeline for quest trigger detection
tasks.description: Create server/services/conversation/quest-trigger-analyzer.ts that inspects each conversation turn for quest-relevant triggers. (1) After each player message, analyze the text for: vocabulary usage (check against quest targetWords), grammar pattern usage (check against quest grammar objectives), topic relevance (NPC-specific quest topics), and task completion phrases. (2) Use keyword matching + fuzzy matching (Levenshtein distance ≤ 2) for vocabulary detection in the target language. (3) Emit quest_progress events via the conversation metadata stream when triggers are detected. (4) Integrate into the HTTP bridge (server/services/conversation/http-bridge.ts) response pipeline so trigger analysis runs on every turn. (5) Return a QUEST_PROGRESS marker block in the response metadata: **QUEST_PROGRESS** ObjectiveId: X, Progress: N/M, Trigger: 'used word X' **END_QUEST_PROGRESS**. (6) Client-side ConversationClient.ts parses this block and updates QuestCompletionEngine.

tasks.title: Define new in-game action types for language learning interactions
tasks.description: Expand the game action system in shared/game-engine/types.ts and create seed actions in a new migration server/migrations/020-language-learning-actions.ts. Add these new action types: (1) 'examine_object' - player inspects a world object and sees its name in target language (key: X or click), (2) 'read_sign' - player reads text on signs, menus, books in target language, (3) 'write_response' - player composes written text in response to a prompt, (4) 'listen_and_repeat' - NPC says a phrase, player must repeat it via speech, (5) 'point_and_name' - player points at object and names it in target language, (6) 'ask_for_directions' - player asks NPC for directions in target language, (7) 'order_food' - player orders at a restaurant/market in target language, (8) 'haggle_price' - player negotiates price in target language, (9) 'introduce_self' - player introduces themselves to NPC, (10) 'describe_scene' - player describes what they see in target language. Add keyboard binding X for examine_object in KeyboardMap.ts.

tasks.title: Implement NPC-initiated conversation system
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCConversationInitiator.ts that allows NPCs to start conversations with the player. (1) NPCs with active quests for the player, or NPCs flagged as mentors/teachers, can initiate conversation when the player is within a configurable proximity radius (default 5 units). (2) Show a speech bubble above the NPC with a greeting in the target language + 'Press G to respond'. (3) If the player doesn't respond within 10 seconds, the NPC calls out again with a different phrase. (4) NPC initiation is governed by a cooldown (minimum 60 seconds between attempts per NPC) and a global cooldown (minimum 30 seconds between any NPC initiation) to avoid overwhelming the player. (5) Priority system: assessment NPCs > quest-giver NPCs > mentor NPCs > ambient NPCs. (6) When conversation starts via NPC initiation, the system prompt includes context that the NPC initiated and should guide the conversation toward their goal (quest offering, assessment, teaching moment). (7) Integrate with NPCAmbientConversationManager.ts to prevent ambient conversations from interrupting initiated ones.

tasks.title: Create adaptive quest generation based on player learning profile
tasks.description: Enhance server/services/quest-generator.ts to generate quests specifically tailored to the player's learning gaps. (1) Load the player's LanguageProgress from shared/language/progress.ts including vocabulary mastery levels, grammar pattern accuracy, and assessment dimension scores. (2) Identify weak areas: vocabulary categories with < 60% mastery, grammar patterns with < 50% accuracy, low-scoring assessment dimensions. (3) Generate quests that specifically target these weak areas - e.g., if 'food vocabulary' is weak, generate a market shopping quest; if grammar accuracy for past tense is low, generate a storytelling quest requiring past tense. (4) Add a new function generateAdaptiveQuest(worldId, playerId) that queries progress data and constructs a targeted quest prompt for Gemini. (5) Include a 'learningObjective' field on the quest schema indicating what skill this quest targets. (6) Balance: 60% quests target weaknesses, 20% reinforce strengths, 20% introduce new material.

tasks.title: Implement NPC exam/quiz system - open conversation assessment
tasks.description: Create server/services/npc-exam-engine.ts for NPCs to administer periodic language exams. Start with 'open conversation' exam type. (1) Define ExamType enum: 'open_conversation', 'question_answer', 'object_recognition', 'writing_prompt', 'reading_comprehension', 'listening_comprehension'. (2) Open conversation exam: NPC initiates a topic-guided conversation (e.g., 'Tell me about your family' or 'Describe your day') and scores the player on vocabulary range, grammar accuracy, fluency, and comprehension using the existing EVAL marker block system from assessment-prompt-utils.ts. (3) Exam triggers: after every 5 quests completed, after 30 minutes of gameplay, or when player requests via NPC interaction. (4) Exam NPC gets a special system prompt that instructs them to probe specific language skills. (5) Store exam results in a new 'exams' subcollection on the player's progress record. (6) Wire exam initiation into NPCConversationInitiator.ts so teacher/professor NPCs can proactively start exams.

tasks.title: Implement NPC exam type: question answering
tasks.description: Add question-answering exam type to server/services/npc-exam-engine.ts. (1) NPC asks the player a series of 5-10 questions in the target language, scaled to CEFR level. (2) A1: simple yes/no and one-word answer questions ('Is this a cat?' 'What color is the sky?'). A2: short answer questions ('Where do you live?' 'What did you eat today?'). B1: open-ended questions requiring multi-sentence answers ('Why do you like this city?' 'What would you do if...'). B2: complex analytical questions ('Compare life in the city vs countryside' 'Explain a tradition from your culture'). (3) Each answer is evaluated by the LLM for correctness, grammar, vocabulary usage, and comprehension. (4) Questions are generated dynamically based on the world context (actual NPCs, locations, items in the settlement) to test practical knowledge. (5) Display questions in the chat panel with a '[Question 3/10]' counter. (6) Final score summary shown at end with breakdown per question.

tasks.title: Implement NPC exam type: object recognition
tasks.description: Add object recognition exam type to server/services/npc-exam-engine.ts. (1) NPC points to or presents objects in the game world and asks 'What is this called?' in the target language. (2) Use the existing 3D world objects (items, buildings, environmental objects) as recognition targets. (3) The exam selects 5-10 objects from the player's vicinity or inventory. (4) For each object, show a visual highlight (glowing outline via Babylon.js HighlightLayer) on the target object while the NPC asks the question. (5) Player must type or speak the name in the target language. (6) Scoring: exact match = full points, close match (Levenshtein ≤ 2) = partial points, wrong = 0. (7) After each answer, NPC provides the correct answer with pronunciation guidance. (8) Objects are selected based on vocabulary the player should know (from learned vocabulary list) plus 2-3 new words to test acquisition. (9) Add a OBJECT_HIGHLIGHT marker block so the server can tell the client which object to highlight.

tasks.title: Implement NPC exam type: writing prompts
tasks.description: Add writing prompt exam type to server/services/npc-exam-engine.ts. (1) NPC gives the player a writing task in the target language: write a short message, letter, description, or story. (2) Prompts scaled by CEFR: A1 = 'Write your name and where you are from' (1-2 sentences), A2 = 'Write a message to a friend about your day' (3-5 sentences), B1 = 'Write a letter to the mayor about a problem in town' (1 paragraph), B2 = 'Write a short story about an adventure' (2-3 paragraphs). (3) Player types their response in a special multi-line text input (expand BabylonChatPanel input to support multi-line mode when in writing exam). (4) LLM evaluates: grammar accuracy, vocabulary range, coherence, task completion, spelling. (5) NPC provides detailed feedback with corrections highlighted. (6) Store the written response and evaluation in the exam record for portfolio review.

tasks.title: Implement NPC exam type: reading comprehension
tasks.description: Add reading comprehension exam type to server/services/npc-exam-engine.ts. (1) NPC presents a text passage in the target language (displayed in the chat panel as a formatted block). (2) Passages are generated by Gemini based on the world context and player's CEFR level. A1: 2-3 simple sentences about a familiar topic. A2: short paragraph (50-80 words) about daily life. B1: 1-2 paragraphs (100-150 words) about a local event or story. B2: longer passage (200+ words) with nuance and cultural context. (3) After reading, NPC asks 3-5 comprehension questions. (4) Questions progress from literal ('What color was the house?') to inferential ('Why did Maria go to the market?') to evaluative ('Do you agree with the character's decision?'). (5) Player answers in the target language. (6) Scoring considers both comprehension accuracy and language quality of the response.

tasks.title: Implement NPC exam type: listening comprehension
tasks.description: Add listening comprehension exam type to server/services/npc-exam-engine.ts. (1) NPC speaks a passage in the target language using TTS (the existing streaming TTS pipeline). (2) The passage is NOT displayed as text - player must listen only. (3) After the passage, NPC asks comprehension questions (these ARE displayed as text). (4) Player can request one replay ('Can you repeat that?') but not more. (5) CEFR scaling: A1 = 1-2 short sentences spoken slowly, A2 = 3-4 sentences at moderate pace, B1 = short monologue at normal pace, B2 = dialogue or complex monologue with idioms. (6) Use the existing TTS system in server/services/tts-stt.ts with adjusted speech rate parameters. (7) Questions test: main idea, specific details, speaker intent, vocabulary in context. (8) Add a 'listening mode' flag to the BabylonChatPanel that hides the NPC text response and only shows an audio waveform visualization.

tasks.title: Convert pre-game assessment into a hardcoded quest structure
tasks.description: Refactor the pre-game (arrival) assessment to use the quest system. Create server/services/assessment-quest-templates.ts. (1) Define a hardcoded 'Arrival Assessment' quest with fixed objectives: meet the greeter NPC (talk_to_npc), complete introduction conversation (complete_conversation with assessment EVAL scoring), demonstrate vocabulary knowledge (use_vocabulary with 5 basic words), navigate to a landmark (visit_location). (2) This quest is auto-assigned when the player first enters the world, before any other quests. (3) The greeter NPC is always the first NPC the player sees (placed at spawn point). (4) Assessment scoring uses the existing EVAL block system but maps results to quest objective completion. (5) Quest completion triggers CEFR level assignment and unlocks the main quest board. (6) Store initial assessment scores on the quest record for later comparison with post-game assessment. (7) The quest is non-skippable and non-abandonable.

tasks.title: Convert post-game assessment into a hardcoded quest structure
tasks.description: Create a hardcoded 'Departure Assessment' quest in server/services/assessment-quest-templates.ts. (1) Define fixed objectives that mirror the arrival assessment for direct comparison: farewell conversation with the greeter NPC, demonstrate expanded vocabulary (use_vocabulary with 10+ words), navigate a more complex route (visit_location chain), complete a mini-exam covering all skills. (2) This quest is triggered when the player indicates they want to finish (via game menu or after completing a threshold of quests). (3) Assessment dimensions (Vocabulary, Grammar, Fluency, Comprehension, TaskCompletion) are scored and compared against arrival scores to compute improvement deltas. (4) Generate a 'Language Learning Report Card' shown as quest completion reward: before/after scores, vocabulary learned, grammar patterns mastered, total conversations, recommended next steps. (5) Quest completion triggers the end-of-game summary screen.

tasks.title: Implement quest feasibility validator against available game actions
tasks.description: Create server/services/quest-feasibility-validator.ts that ensures every generated quest can actually be completed given the current game mechanics. (1) Maintain a registry of completable action types: movement actions (player can walk to locations), conversation actions (player can talk to NPCs), inventory actions (player can pick up/deliver items), vocabulary actions (player can use words in conversation), examination actions (player can examine objects). (2) For each quest objective, verify: the target NPC exists and is reachable, the target location exists and is navigable, required items exist in the world or can be spawned, vocabulary words are in the target language dictionary. (3) Reject or modify quests with infeasible objectives before presenting them to the player. (4) Add a feasibility score (0-1) to each quest indicating how achievable it is. (5) Integrate as a validation step in both quest-generator.ts and quest-assignment-engine.ts, running after generation but before assignment.

tasks.title: Implement NPC-guided conversation mode for quest objectives
tasks.description: Enhance server/services/conversation/context-manager.ts to support 'guided conversation' mode when the player is working on a conversation-based quest objective. (1) When the player has an active quest with a complete_conversation or use_vocabulary objective involving the current NPC, inject guidance into the NPC's system prompt. (2) The NPC should steer the conversation toward the quest topic naturally (not abruptly). (3) If the player goes off-topic, the NPC gently redirects: 'That's interesting, but I was wondering about...' (4) The NPC should use scaffolding techniques: ask leading questions, provide sentence starters, offer word hints when the player struggles (detected by long pauses or off-target responses). (5) Add a 'conversationGoal' field to the quest objective that specifies what the NPC should guide toward. (6) Track conversation turns and if the player hasn't made progress in 5 turns, increase hint frequency.

tasks.title: Implement quest chain system for progressive learning
tasks.description: Create server/services/quest-chain-manager.ts for multi-part quest sequences that build on each other. (1) Define a QuestChain type with ordered quest references, unlock conditions, and a chain-level reward. (2) Each quest in a chain can specify prerequisiteQuestIds that must be completed before it unlocks. (3) Create starter chains: 'First Words' (5 quests progressing from greetings → self-introduction → asking questions → describing things → telling a story), 'Market Day' (3 quests: learn food vocabulary → visit market → complete a purchase), 'Town Explorer' (4 quests: learn direction words → follow directions → give directions → navigate independently). (4) Chain progress is visible in the quest log with a chain progress bar. (5) Completing a full chain grants bonus XP and a special achievement. (6) Add schema field 'chainId' and 'chainOrder' to quest schema in shared/schema.ts.

tasks.title: Add examine_object action with target-language labeling
tasks.description: Implement the 'examine_object' action in client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts. (1) When player presses X near a world object (item, building, environmental prop), display a floating label showing the object's name in the target language with pronunciation guide. (2) Use Babylon.js GUI Label positioned above the object. (3) Label shows: target language name (large), romanization/transliteration (medium), English translation (small, faded). (4) Label persists for 5 seconds then fades. (5) First examination of an object adds it to the player's vocabulary list via the existing vocabulary tracking in LanguageProgress. (6) Subsequent examinations show a 'Known ✓' badge. (7) Objects need a targetLanguageName field populated during world generation - add this to the item schema and populate it via server/generators/world-generator.ts using the settlement's primary language. (8) Fire a 'vocabulary_examined' event on GameEventBus for quest trigger detection.

tasks.title: Add read_sign action for environmental text interaction
tasks.description: Implement 'read_sign' action for interacting with text in the game world. (1) Buildings, shops, and landmarks display signs with text in the target language. (2) When the player approaches a sign and presses X, show a close-up panel with the sign text in target language, word-by-word translation on hover, and full English translation toggle. (3) Signs include: shop names and descriptions, street signs, notice board postings, menu boards outside restaurants, directional signs. (4) Each sign interaction logs which words the player encountered and whether they used the translation toggle. (5) Sign content is generated during settlement creation using the target language via the existing tracery grammar system in server/services/tracery-service.ts. (6) Add a 'signs' array to settlement data with position, text, and translation. (7) Fire 'sign_read' event for quest tracking. (8) Create reusable Babylon.js GUI panel component for sign display.

tasks.title: Add point_and_name action for vocabulary practice
tasks.description: Implement 'point_and_name' action allowing players to point at objects and name them. (1) When holding the P key, show a crosshair/pointer cursor. (2) The crosshair highlights objects the player looks at (raycast from camera). (3) When the player releases P while targeting an object, open a small input prompt: 'What is this called in [target language]?' (4) Player types or speaks their answer. (5) Compare answer against the object's targetLanguageName using fuzzy matching (Levenshtein ≤ 2 for partial credit, exact match for full credit). (6) Show immediate feedback: green flash + chime for correct, gentle correction with the right answer for incorrect. (7) Award vocabulary XP for correct answers, reduced XP for close answers. (8) Track accuracy over time per object/word. (9) Fire 'object_named' event for quest objective tracking. (10) Add P key binding to KeyboardMap.ts.

tasks.title: Implement vocabulary-focused quest objectives with spaced repetition
tasks.description: Create a spaced repetition layer for vocabulary quest objectives in server/services/quest-generator.ts. (1) Track when each vocabulary word was last practiced and how many times it was correctly used (already partially in LanguageProgress.vocabulary). (2) Calculate review intervals using a simplified SM-2 algorithm: new words review after 1 quest, then 3, then 7, then 14 quests. (3) When generating vocabulary-focused quests, prioritize words due for review. (4) New quest objective type concept: 'review_vocabulary' - player must correctly use N words from their review queue in conversations. (5) Map this to the existing 'use_vocabulary' canonical type but populate targetWords from the spaced repetition queue rather than random selection. (6) Track repetition state in LanguageProgress: add 'interval', 'easeFactor', 'nextReviewAt' fields per VocabularyEntry. (7) Generate review quests automatically when the player has 5+ words due for review.

tasks.title: Implement quest rewards tied to language skill progression
tasks.description: Enhance quest completion rewards in server/routes.ts POST /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/:questId/complete. (1) Beyond XP, quests now award specific language skill boosts: vocabulary quests grant vocabulary dimension points, grammar quests grant grammar dimension points, conversation quests grant fluency points, comprehension quests grant comprehension points. (2) Define reward scaling: quest difficulty × objective count × performance multiplier (based on how well objectives were completed, not just pass/fail). (3) Performance multiplier: completed on first try = 1.5x, used hints = 1.0x, required multiple attempts = 0.75x. (4) Award bonus for completing quests in target language immersion mode (>80% target language in conversations). (5) Update LanguageProgress.assessmentDimensions when rewards are applied. (6) Show a detailed reward breakdown in the quest completion UI. (7) Unlock new vocabulary categories or grammar patterns as rewards for chain completion.

tasks.title: Create daily/recurring quest system for consistent practice
tasks.description: Add a daily quest system in server/services/daily-quest-generator.ts. (1) Each game day (configurable, default = 20 minutes real time), generate 3 daily quests: one vocabulary practice, one conversation challenge, one exploration task. (2) Daily quests are simpler than main quests (1-2 objectives each) and designed for quick completion (5-10 minutes). (3) Completing all 3 daily quests grants a streak bonus (increasing XP multiplier for consecutive days). (4) Daily quests rotate through different vocabulary categories and grammar patterns to ensure broad coverage. (5) Track daily quest completion streak in LanguageProgress.streakDays. (6) Show daily quests in a dedicated section of the quest log with a countdown timer. (7) If the player misses a day, the streak resets but they don't lose progress. (8) Wire into the existing quest assignment endpoint or add a new GET /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/daily endpoint.

tasks.title: Implement quest hints system with scaffolded assistance
tasks.description: Add a hint system for when players are stuck on quest objectives. (1) Each quest objective has up to 3 hint levels: Level 1 = vague directional hint ('Try visiting the market area'), Level 2 = specific guidance ('Talk to the baker about bread'), Level 3 = explicit instruction ('Say "pan" to the baker when she asks what you want'). (2) Player requests hints via a 'Hint' button in the quest checklist UI (costs a small XP penalty, reduces performance multiplier). (3) Hints are generated at quest creation time by Gemini as part of the quest generation prompt. (4) For vocabulary objectives, Level 3 hints include the actual target words with pronunciation. (5) For location objectives, Level 2+ hints add a waypoint marker on the minimap. (6) Track hint usage per objective for performance scoring. (7) NPCs can also give contextual hints during conversation if they detect the player is struggling with a quest objective involving them.

tasks.title: Implement automatic quest completion detection and celebration
tasks.description: Wire the QuestCompletionEngine into the game loop for automatic detection when all objectives are met. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts, add a periodic check (every 2 seconds) that evaluates all active quest objectives via the QuestCompletionEngine. (2) When all objectives for a quest are completed, trigger a celebration sequence: confetti particle effect, level-up sound, quest completion banner ('Quest Complete: [Quest Title]'). (3) Show the reward breakdown overlay for 5 seconds. (4) If the quest has a turn-in NPC, mark the NPC's indicator as ✓ (green) and prompt 'Return to [NPC] to claim your reward'. (5) If no turn-in NPC, auto-complete immediately. (6) Fire 'quest_completed' event on GameEventBus. (7) Update the server immediately via POST /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/:questId/complete. (8) For quest chains, immediately unlock the next quest and show 'New Quest Available!' notification.

tasks.title: Add NPC memory of past quest interactions
tasks.description: Enhance server/services/conversation/context-manager.ts to include quest history in NPC conversation context. (1) When building the NPC context, include a summary of quests this NPC has given the player and their completion status. (2) NPCs reference past quests naturally: 'Last time you helped me find those herbs, remember?' (3) If the player failed or abandoned a quest from this NPC, the NPC can offer it again with encouragement: 'Would you like to try again? I can give you some hints this time.' (4) NPCs who have given the player successful quests have increased trust/friendship, reflected in conversation tone. (5) Store quest-NPC associations in the quest record (assignerNpcId field). (6) Limit context to last 5 quests per NPC to stay within token budget. (7) This creates a sense of persistent relationship between player and quest-giving NPCs.

tasks.title: Implement context-aware quest generation using world state
tasks.description: Enhance server/services/quest-generator.ts to use rich world context when generating quests. (1) Load the full settlement data: all NPCs with occupations, all locations with types, all items, current time of day, weather, active events. (2) Generate quests that reference actual game entities: 'Go to [real bakery name] and order [real menu item] from [real baker NPC name]'. (3) Ensure generated NPC names match actual NPCs via the existing charName() helper. (4) Ensure location references match actual settlement locations. (5) If a quest mentions an item, ensure that item exists in the world or can be created. (6) Add seasonal/time-based quest variations: morning quests involve market/breakfast vocabulary, evening quests involve dinner/social vocabulary. (7) Include cultural context from the world's language and setting for culturally authentic quest content.

tasks.title: Add quest difficulty indicators and CEFR alignment
tasks.description: Enhance the quest UI to clearly communicate difficulty and language level requirements. (1) Each quest shows a CEFR badge (A1, A2, B1, B2) indicating the language level required. (2) Display difficulty stars (1-5) based on objective complexity and vocabulary difficulty. (3) Color-code quests in the quest log: green = at or below player level, yellow = slightly above, red = significantly above. (4) Add a 'Recommended' tag to quests matching the player's current CEFR level. (5) Show estimated time to complete (based on objective count and type). (6) In the quest assignment engine, filter quests by player's CEFR level ± 1 (can attempt slightly above or below). (7) Tooltip on difficulty indicator explains what language skills are needed. (8) Update QuestIndicatorManager.ts to color the NPC quest icon (!) based on quest difficulty relative to player level.

tasks.title: Implement grammar-focused quest objectives
tasks.description: Add grammar practice as a first-class quest objective concept. (1) Create grammar-focused quest templates in shared/language/quest-templates.ts: 'Past Tense Tales' (tell an NPC about yesterday using past tense), 'Question Master' (ask 5 questions to NPCs using correct question formation), 'Polite Requests' (make formal requests at 3 different shops). (2) Quest trigger analyzer detects grammar pattern usage by sending the player's text through the existing grammar feedback pipeline. (3) Track which grammar patterns the player used correctly vs incorrectly during quest conversations. (4) Map to existing canonical types: complete_conversation with a grammar focus flag, or use_vocabulary with grammar patterns as the target. (5) NPC provides inline grammar corrections during quest conversations without breaking immersion ('Ah, you mean [corrected form]? Yes, that's right!'). (6) Add grammar pattern tracking per quest objective.

tasks.title: Implement role-play scenario quests
tasks.description: Create structured role-play quests in server/services/quest-generator.ts where the player must play a specific social role. (1) Scenario types: 'At the Doctor' (describe symptoms), 'Job Interview' (answer questions about skills), 'Lost Tourist' (ask for directions), 'Restaurant Patron' (order a meal), 'New Neighbor' (introduce yourself to the neighborhood), 'Market Haggler' (negotiate prices). (2) Each scenario has a goal, required vocabulary set, expected dialogue patterns, and success criteria. (3) The NPC plays their complementary role (doctor, interviewer, local guide, waiter, neighbor, vendor) with appropriate dialogue. (4) Success is measured by: completing the social transaction, using required vocabulary, maintaining the role appropriately. (5) CEFR-scaled: A1 scenarios have very constrained vocabulary and short exchanges; B2 scenarios require nuanced negotiation and complex expression. (6) Add 'scenario_type' field to quest objectives.

tasks.title: Implement quest log with filtering and statistics
tasks.description: Overhaul the quest log panel (J key) in BabylonGame.ts. (1) Three tabs: Active Quests, Completed Quests, Quest Stats. (2) Active tab shows quest checklists with real-time progress (from task 2). (3) Completed tab shows historical quests with completion date, performance rating (stars), and language skills practiced. (4) Stats tab shows: total quests completed, completion rate, average performance, vocabulary words learned through quests, grammar patterns practiced, CEFR progress over time (line chart). (5) Filter by: quest type (vocabulary, grammar, conversation, exploration), difficulty level, NPC giver, completion status. (6) Sort by: most recent, difficulty, reward value. (7) Search by quest title or keyword. (8) Each completed quest expands to show detailed breakdown: objectives completed, time taken, hints used, vocabulary used, grammar accuracy.

tasks.title: Implement NPC mentor system for guided learning
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCMentorSystem.ts for a dedicated mentor NPC relationship. (1) When the player enters the world, they are assigned a mentor NPC (teacher, professor, or elder character). (2) The mentor NPC is always available at a known location (school, library, or town square). (3) Mentor provides: quest recommendations ('I think you should try talking to the baker next'), learning tips ('Remember, in [language], adjectives come after nouns'), progress check-ins ('You've learned 20 new words this session! Great work!'). (4) Mentor initiates conversation when the player returns to their location after completing quests. (5) Mentor can assign custom quests based on observed player weaknesses. (6) Mentor relationship deepens over time, unlocking more advanced teaching. (7) Add 'isMentor' flag to NPC and 'mentorNpcId' to player session. (8) Mentor conversations have a special system prompt emphasizing pedagogical approach.

tasks.title: Add scavenger hunt quest type for vocabulary collection
tasks.description: Create a scavenger hunt quest template that combines exploration with vocabulary building. (1) Quest gives the player a list of 5-10 items to find and name in the target language. (2) Items are scattered across the settlement at logical locations (food at market, tools at blacksmith, books at library). (3) Player must physically navigate to each item location and use the examine_object or point_and_name action. (4) The quest checklist shows items as a visual grid with silhouettes that fill in when found and correctly named. (5) Bonus objective: find all items within a time limit. (6) CEFR scaling: A1 = common objects with picture hints, A2 = everyday items with text-only clues, B1 = abstract concepts with riddle clues, B2 = idiomatic expressions tied to locations. (7) Add a minimap overlay showing search zones (not exact locations) as hints. (8) Integrate with the spaced repetition system to include review words.

tasks.title: Implement conversation quality scoring for quest objectives
tasks.description: Create server/services/conversation-quality-scorer.ts to evaluate conversation quality during quest objectives. (1) For complete_conversation objectives, don't just check 'did they talk?' but 'how well did they talk?' (2) Scoring dimensions: vocabulary richness (unique words / total words), grammar accuracy (errors per sentence), target language ratio (% of words in target language vs English), conversation depth (multi-turn exchanges vs single-turn), topic relevance (stayed on quest topic). (3) Each dimension scored 0-100, combined into an overall quality score. (4) Quest objective completion thresholds: minimum 40% quality to pass, 70% for full credit, 90%+ for bonus. (5) Quality score affects quest rewards via performance multiplier. (6) Show quality breakdown in quest completion summary. (7) Store conversation quality data for learning analytics. (8) Use the existing EVAL marker block data when available, supplement with text analysis when not.

tasks.title: Implement quest notification and reminder system
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/QuestNotificationSystem.ts for timely quest-related notifications. (1) Toast notifications (top-right corner, auto-dismiss after 5 seconds): 'New Quest Available!', 'Objective Complete!', 'Quest Updated', 'Daily Quests Reset'. (2) Proximity reminders: when player walks near a quest-relevant NPC or location, show subtle reminder ('Quest: Talk to [NPC] nearby'). (3) Idle reminders: if the player hasn't made quest progress in 5 minutes, show a gentle nudge ('Don't forget: you have 2 active quests. Press J to view.'). (4) Achievement-style popups for milestones: first quest complete, 5 quests complete, first chain complete, first perfect score. (5) Configurable notification frequency (none/minimal/normal/all) in game settings. (6) Audio cues: subtle chime for objective progress, triumphant jingle for quest completion. (7) Integrate with GameEventBus for event-driven notification triggers.

tasks.title: Implement quest abandonment, failure, and retry mechanics
tasks.description: Add quest abandonment and retry handling. (1) Player can abandon any quest from the quest log (except hardcoded assessment quests) via an 'Abandon' button. (2) Abandoned quests move to a 'Failed/Abandoned' section with the option to retry. (3) Retry resets all objective progress but keeps hint state (player remembers previous hints). (4) Time-limited quests (daily quests) auto-fail when the timer expires. (5) Conversation-based objectives can fail if the player ends the conversation prematurely (before quality threshold met). (6) Failed objectives show a red X instead of empty checkbox. (7) The quest-giving NPC acknowledges failure gracefully: 'No worries, you can try again when you're ready.' (8) Track attempt count per quest. (9) After 3 failed attempts, auto-reduce quest difficulty or offer simplified objectives. (10) Add PATCH /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/:questId/abandon endpoint.

tasks.title: Add shopping and economic vocabulary quests
tasks.description: Create quest templates for shopping/economic interactions in shared/language/quest-templates.ts. (1) 'Market Shopping' quest: player given a shopping list of 3-5 items in the target language, must visit merchant NPCs and request items by name. (2) 'Price Negotiation' quest: player must haggle with vendor NPC, using number vocabulary and comparison phrases ('too expensive', 'can you lower the price?'). (3) 'Restaurant Order' quest: player reads a menu (in target language), orders a meal, asks about ingredients. (4) 'Currency Exchange' quest: player practices number vocabulary by counting money and making change. (5) Each template includes required vocabulary lists and grammar patterns. (6) Merchant NPCs respond appropriately to correct/incorrect vocabulary. (7) Quest completion requires actually 'purchasing' items (deducting from player's gold) which adds them to inventory. (8) Items purchased can be delivered to other NPCs for follow-up quests.

tasks.title: Add direction-giving and navigation quests
tasks.description: Create quest templates for direction/navigation language practice. (1) 'Follow the Guide' quest: NPC gives verbal directions in target language ('Turn left at the bakery, walk straight to the fountain, the library is on your right'), player must follow them to reach the destination. (2) 'Guide the Stranger' quest: lost NPC asks for directions, player must give correct directions in target language using learned vocabulary (left, right, straight, near, far, next to). (3) 'Treasure Map' quest: player receives written directions in target language and must decode them to find a location. (4) Direction vocabulary is introduced progressively: A1 = left/right/straight, A2 = near/far/between/behind, B1 = complex instructions with landmarks, B2 = giving elaborate directions with alternatives. (5) Use the actual settlement street network from StreetNetworkLayout.ts for realistic navigation. (6) Map follow_directions and navigate_language canonical objective types to these templates.

tasks.title: Add storytelling and narrative quests
tasks.description: Create quest templates for narrative/storytelling practice. (1) 'Story Circle' quest: player joins a group of NPCs and must contribute to a collaborative story in the target language (each participant adds a sentence). (2) 'Local Legend' quest: NPC tells a folk tale, player must retell it to another NPC (tests listening comprehension + oral retelling). (3) 'My Adventure' quest: player must describe their in-game experiences to an NPC journalist/scribe using past tense and descriptive vocabulary. (4) 'Picture This' quest: player sees a scene (game vista) and must describe it to an NPC who can't see it. (5) Storytelling quests emphasize: past tense, sequencing words (first, then, finally), descriptive adjectives, emotional vocabulary. (6) NPC audience reacts to the story quality with encouragement or gentle corrections. (7) Performance scored on vocabulary range, grammar accuracy, narrative coherence.

tasks.title: Implement social relationship quests
tasks.description: Create quest templates focused on building NPC relationships through language use. (1) 'Make a Friend' quest: player must have 3 successful conversations with a specific NPC, progressing from introduction → shared interests → making plans. (2) 'Community Helper' quest: player assists multiple NPCs with small tasks (deliver messages, explain things, translate for a visiting NPC). (3) 'Peacemaker' quest: two NPCs are in a disagreement, player must mediate using polite language, conditional tense, and persuasive vocabulary. (4) 'Party Planner' quest: player invites NPCs to an event, requiring invitation language, polite requests, and time/place vocabulary. (5) Social quests build on the existing relationship system (friendship, trust levels in context-manager.ts). (6) Completing social quests increases relationship scores, which unlocks deeper conversations and more complex quests from that NPC. (7) Track unique social interactions per NPC.

tasks.title: Implement cultural immersion quests
tasks.description: Create quest templates that teach cultural knowledge alongside language. (1) 'Festival Day' quest: player participates in a cultural celebration, learning associated vocabulary (holiday greetings, traditional food names, ceremonial phrases). (2) 'Recipe Quest' quest: NPC teaches player a traditional recipe, player must follow instructions in target language (imperative verb forms, cooking vocabulary, measurements). (3) 'Cultural Exchange' quest: player explains aspects of their own culture to an NPC, then learns about the local culture (comparison language, 'In my country we...'). (4) 'Proverb Hunter' quest: player collects local proverbs from elder NPCs and must explain their meaning. (5) 'Traditional Craft' quest: NPC demonstrates a craft, player must describe the process. (6) Cultural quests introduce: formal/informal registers, idiomatic expressions, culturally-specific vocabulary. (7) Use world cultural settings from world-generator.ts to ensure cultural authenticity.

tasks.title: Add describe_scene action for visual vocabulary practice
tasks.description: Implement the 'describe_scene' action in BabylonGame.ts. (1) Player presses a key (bind to B in KeyboardMap.ts) to enter 'description mode'. (2) A screenshot/snapshot of the current view is taken (Babylon.js CreateScreenshot). (3) The scene is analyzed to identify visible objects, NPCs, buildings, and environmental features using the game's scene graph (not computer vision - enumerate rendered meshes and their semantic tags). (4) Player is prompted: 'Describe what you see in [target language]'. (5) Player types or speaks their description. (6) LLM evaluates the description against known scene contents: did they mention visible objects? Did they use correct vocabulary? Did they use spatial language correctly (left, right, in front, behind)? (7) Score on: completeness (% of objects mentioned), vocabulary accuracy, grammar quality, spatial language use. (8) Fire 'scene_described' event for quest tracking.

tasks.title: Implement real-time quest objective progress events via GameEventBus
tasks.description: Ensure all game actions fire appropriate events for quest tracking. (1) Define a standardized set of quest-relevant events on GameEventBus: 'player:visited_location', 'player:talked_to_npc', 'player:used_vocabulary', 'player:collected_item', 'player:examined_object', 'player:read_sign', 'player:named_object', 'player:described_scene', 'player:completed_conversation', 'player:gave_directions'. (2) Each event payload includes: eventType, timestamp, context (NPC name, location, words used, etc.). (3) QuestCompletionEngine listens for these events and checks them against all active quest objectives. (4) When an event matches an objective, immediately update progress and emit 'quest:objective_progress' event. (5) Ensure events are also sent to the server for persistence via a batched API call (debounced, max every 5 seconds). (6) Add missing event emissions to existing actions (movement, conversation, inventory).

tasks.title: Add quest waypoint markers on minimap
tasks.description: Enhance client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonMinimap.ts to show quest objective waypoints. (1) Active quest objectives with location targets show as colored markers on the minimap: yellow diamond for visit_location, blue person icon for talk_to_npc, green item for collect_item. (2) The closest/most relevant quest objective marker pulses gently to guide the player. (3) Quest waypoints only appear for the player's tracked quest (one quest can be 'tracked' at a time, toggled in quest log). (4) Distance text shows below each marker (e.g., '45m'). (5) Clicking a minimap marker sets it as the navigation target, showing a dotted path line on the minimap. (6) Turn-in NPCs show as golden star markers. (7) Exam/assessment locations show as book icon markers. (8) Markers respect fog of war if implemented, only showing for explored areas.

tasks.title: Implement teaching-back quests where player teaches NPC
tasks.description: Create quest templates where the player takes on a teaching role. (1) 'Teach the Child' quest: a young NPC wants to learn words the player has already mastered, player must correctly teach them (use the word in context, provide examples, correct the NPC's intentional mistakes). (2) 'Translation Helper' quest: a visiting NPC doesn't speak the local language, player must translate between them and a local NPC. (3) 'Pronunciation Coach' quest: NPC struggles with pronunciation, player must model correct pronunciation via speech input. (4) Teaching quests test the highest level of mastery - if you can teach it, you know it. (5) The 'student' NPC deliberately makes mistakes that the player must catch and correct. (6) Only assign teaching quests for vocabulary/grammar the player has achieved 'familiar' or 'mastered' level. (7) Successful teaching reinforces the player's own knowledge (boost spaced repetition interval).

tasks.title: Implement emergency/situational quests for practical language
tasks.description: Create time-sensitive situational quests that test practical language skills. (1) 'Lost and Found' quest: player receives a found item and must ask NPCs if it belongs to them, describing the item in target language. (2) 'Medical Emergency' quest: an NPC is feeling ill, player must communicate symptoms to the healer using body part and symptom vocabulary. (3) 'Missing Person' quest: an NPC's family member is missing, player must describe the missing person to guards and ask townspeople if they've seen them. (4) 'Weather Warning' quest: player must warn NPCs about incoming bad weather, using weather vocabulary and imperative forms. (5) Situational quests have a soft time pressure (not instant fail, but bonus XP for quick completion). (6) These quests are triggered semi-randomly during gameplay for variety. (7) Required vocabulary is practical/survival-oriented, high priority in real language learning.

tasks.title: Add multi-line writing interface for composition quests
tasks.description: Enhance BabylonChatPanel.ts input to support a multi-line writing mode for composition tasks. (1) When a quest objective or exam requires extended writing, the chat input expands into a larger text area (5-10 lines visible). (2) Show a word count and character count at the bottom. (3) Add basic text formatting hints (but no rich text - just character count guidance). (4) Writing mode includes a 'Submit' button separate from the normal send action. (5) A small sidebar shows: quest prompt, required word count range, vocabulary suggestions (optional toggle). (6) Auto-save drafts every 30 seconds in case of accidental closure. (7) After submission, the NPC responds with detailed feedback displayed in a formatted evaluation panel. (8) Toggle between normal chat mode and writing mode seamlessly.

tasks.title: Implement pronunciation quest objectives with speech scoring
tasks.description: Enhance pronunciation-related quest objectives using the existing STT pipeline. (1) For pronunciation_check objective type, require the player to speak specific words/phrases via push-to-talk (R key). (2) Compare STT transcription against the expected phrase - exact match = perfect, partial match = partial credit. (3) NPC models the correct pronunciation first (via TTS), then the player repeats. (4) Repeat cycle: NPC says → Player repeats → Score → NPC corrects if needed → Player tries again (up to 3 attempts). (5) Track pronunciation accuracy per word over time in LanguageProgress. (6) Pronunciation quests focus on commonly mispronounced sounds specific to the target language. (7) Visual feedback: show the expected text and the transcribed text side-by-side so the player can see the difference. (8) Award mastery badges for consistently correct pronunciation.

tasks.title: Implement NPC schedule-aware quest assignment
tasks.description: Integrate quest assignment with the NPC schedule system in client/src/components/3DGame/NPCScheduleSystem.ts. (1) When generating quests that reference specific NPCs, check the NPC's schedule to ensure they'll be at the referenced location when the player attempts the quest. (2) Quest descriptions include contextual time references: 'Visit Maria at the bakery in the morning' (when her schedule has her there). (3) If a quest NPC is not at their expected location, show a hint: '[NPC] is usually at [location] during [time period]'. (4) Time-of-day quest variants: morning quests (market, breakfast), afternoon quests (work, errands), evening quests (tavern, social). (5) Quest feasibility validator checks NPC schedule compatibility. (6) Special quests for following an NPC's daily routine, learning time-of-day vocabulary and routine description language.

tasks.title: Add quest branching based on player choices during conversations
tasks.description: Implement quest branching where player dialogue choices affect quest progression. (1) Define a BranchingObjective type that includes conditional outcomes: if the player says X, objective A unlocks; if they say Y, objective B unlocks instead. (2) Example: 'Market Quest' - if player haggles successfully, bonus objective 'Negotiate with 2 more vendors' unlocks; if they pay full price, 'Earn more gold' objective appears instead. (3) Branching is detected by the conversation trigger analyzer examining player responses. (4) Maximum 2 branch points per quest to keep complexity manageable. (5) All branches lead to quest completion but via different paths (no dead ends). (6) Branch choices are recorded for learning analytics (which language strategies does the player prefer?). (7) UI shows branching visually in quest checklist with a fork icon. (8) Add 'branchCondition' and 'branchOutcomes' fields to quest objective schema.

tasks.title: Implement quest recommendation engine
tasks.description: Create server/services/quest-recommendation-engine.ts that suggests the most beneficial quests for the player. (1) Score available quests based on: learning gap coverage (how much does this quest target weak areas?), spaced repetition needs (does this quest review due vocabulary?), variety (avoid repeating similar quest types consecutively), difficulty appropriateness (within CEFR ± 0.5 level), completion history (deprioritize recently completed similar quests). (2) Return top 3 recommended quests with explanations: 'Recommended because you need practice with food vocabulary'. (3) Mentor NPC uses recommendations when suggesting quests. (4) Quest board UI shows recommended quests first with a star icon. (5) Factor in player engagement: if they prefer conversation quests, weight those slightly higher but still ensure coverage of all skill areas. (6) Update recommendations after each quest completion.

tasks.title: Add conversation_initiation objective for NPC-proactive quests
tasks.description: Map a new quest flow where the NPC initiates and the player must respond appropriately. (1) For objectives where the NPC starts the conversation, add a 'npcInitiated' flag on the quest objective. (2) When this flag is set, the NPCConversationInitiator triggers the conversation and the quest objective tracks the player's response quality. (3) Example quest: 'A stranger approaches you and asks for help. Respond appropriately in [target language].' (4) The NPC's opening line is specified in the objective and the player must respond coherently. (5) Conversation quality scorer evaluates: did the player understand the NPC's request? Did they respond in the target language? Was the response contextually appropriate? (6) This tests receptive skills (understanding unprompted speech) rather than just productive skills (initiating conversation). (7) Map to existing talk_to_npc and complete_conversation canonical types with the npcInitiated modifier.

tasks.title: Implement quest completion portfolio and learning journal
tasks.description: Create a persistent learning journal that aggregates quest performance into a player portfolio. (1) New UI panel accessible from the game menu: 'Learning Journal'. (2) Shows: vocabulary words learned (grouped by category with mastery levels), grammar patterns practiced (with accuracy trends), conversation logs with quality scores, quest performance history with improvement graphs. (3) CEFR progress tracker: visual meter showing progress toward next level with specific criteria needed. (4) 'Achievements' section: badges for milestones (First Conversation, Vocabulary Collector 50/100/200, Grammar Guru, Cultural Explorer, etc.). (5) Export functionality: generate a PDF report of learning progress for teachers/researchers. (6) Store portfolio data in LanguageProgress extended with new fields. (7) Portfolio is referenced by the departure assessment quest to show improvement.

tasks.title: Implement timed challenge quests
tasks.description: Create time-limited challenge quests for intensive practice. (1) 'Speed Round' quest: player has 3 minutes to correctly name as many objects as possible using point_and_name. (2) 'Rapid Conversation' quest: player has 5 minutes to have meaningful exchanges with 3 different NPCs. (3) 'Vocabulary Sprint' quest: player has 2 minutes to use 10 specific vocabulary words in conversation. (4) Timer displayed prominently in the HUD during timed quests. (5) Scoring based on completion count within time limit (no pass/fail, just performance tier: bronze/silver/gold). (6) Time pressure is gentler at A1-A2 (longer limits) and more challenging at B1-B2. (7) Optional: player can retry timed quests immediately to beat their previous score. (8) Timed quests are always optional, never blocking main quest progression. (9) Leaderboard stored locally showing personal best for each timed challenge.

tasks.title: Add item interaction quests with language-gated crafting
tasks.description: Create quests that require language-mediated item interactions. (1) 'Recipe Crafting' quest: player collects ingredients (named in target language) and must request a craftsperson NPC to combine them, describing what they want in target language. (2) 'Tool Identification' quest: blacksmith lays out tools, player must correctly name each one to borrow it. (3) 'Potion Making' quest: herbalist gives verbal instructions in target language for combining ingredients in specific order. (4) Item interaction requires the player to name or describe items correctly before they can use them. (5) Use existing inventory system from BabylonInventory.ts for item management. (6) Items have a 'requiresLanguageCheck' flag; when true, player must name the item correctly before using it. (7) Incorrect naming shows the item name briefly as a learning moment. (8) Track item vocabulary separately for practical/functional vocabulary metrics.

tasks.title: Implement multi-NPC quest scenarios
tasks.description: Create quests that require interacting with multiple NPCs in sequence or simultaneously. (1) 'Message Relay' quest: player receives a message from NPC A in target language, must accurately relay it to NPC B (tests listening comprehension + speaking). (2) 'Dinner Party' quest: player must invite 3 NPCs to dinner by visiting each and extending an invitation, then host a group conversation. (3) 'Town Meeting' quest: player moderates a discussion between disagreeing NPCs, restating each person's position. (4) Multi-NPC quests track per-NPC interaction completion in the checklist. (5) Conversations reference information from prior NPC interactions: NPC B asks 'What did [NPC A] say about this?'. (6) Implement via QuestCompletionEngine tracking multiple talk_to_npc objectives with ordered dependencies. (7) These quests naturally introduce varied vocabulary and conversation styles since each NPC has unique personality and speech patterns.

tasks.title: Add ambient vocabulary reinforcement during exploration
tasks.description: Create passive vocabulary exposure during normal gameplay exploration. (1) As the player walks through the settlement, objects in their field of view occasionally display floating target-language labels (not all at once - 1-2 at a time, cycling). (2) Labels appear for objects the player has encountered in quests but hasn't mastered yet (from spaced repetition queue). (3) Labels are subtle and semi-transparent to avoid cluttering the view. (4) Can be toggled on/off in settings (default: on for A1-A2, off for B1+). (5) Clicking a floating label triggers a mini-interaction: hear pronunciation, see example sentence. (6) Track which ambient labels the player interacted with for spaced repetition data. (7) This creates passive learning even when the player isn't actively doing quests. (8) Implement using Babylon.js GUI labels with distance-based fade. (9) Labels prioritize words the player will need for their current active quest.

tasks.title: Implement listen_and_repeat action for pronunciation practice
tasks.description: Add a listen-and-repeat interaction mode. (1) During conversations with NPCs, if the player types '/repeat' or presses a repeat button, the NPC repeats their last phrase slowly. (2) Player then uses push-to-talk (R) to repeat the phrase. (3) STT transcription is compared against the original phrase. (4) Feedback shown: matched words in green, missed/wrong words in red, with correct version alongside. (5) NPC encourages: 'Good try! Listen again...' or 'Perfect pronunciation!' (6) Can be used outside of quests for general practice, but quest objectives of type pronunciation_check specifically require it. (7) Repeat requests don't count as conversation turns (don't affect conversation quality score negatively). (8) Track repeat attempts and improvement over time per phrase/word.

tasks.title: Add order_food action for restaurant interactions
tasks.description: Implement a restaurant/market ordering interaction in BabylonGame.ts. (1) When the player enters a restaurant or market building (tagged as such in the settlement data), show a menu board in the target language. (2) Player can interact with the vendor NPC and use the 'order_food' action by saying the item name in the target language. (3) The vendor NPC responds contextually: correct order = 'Right away!' + item added to inventory, close but wrong = 'Did you mean [correct]?', completely wrong = 'I don't understand, could you try again?'. (4) Menu items have target-language names, prices, and optional descriptions. (5) Ordering requires saying the item name, quantity (number practice), and polite phrasing ('I would like...' / 'please'). (6) Track successful orders for vocabulary and transaction vocabulary mastery. (7) Link to shopping quests for seamless integration.

tasks.title: Implement ask_for_directions action with NPC pathfinding response
tasks.description: Add an 'ask for directions' interaction. (1) Player can ask any NPC for directions to a known location by typing or speaking: 'Where is the [location]?' in the target language. (2) NPC responds with verbal directions using the actual street network: 'Go straight down [street name], turn left at [landmark], it's the [nth] building on the right.' (3) Directions are generated using the settlement's StreetNetworkLayout.ts to compute an actual path between the player's position and the target. (4) After NPC gives directions, optional waypoint markers appear on minimap (configurable difficulty: show waypoints at A1-A2, hide at B1+). (5) Player must recognize direction vocabulary to follow correctly. (6) If player asks in English instead of target language, NPC gently prompts: 'Can you ask me in [target language]?' (7) Fire 'asked_directions' event for quest tracking. (8) Direction accuracy scored for quests requiring this action.

tasks.title: Add introduce_self action for social interaction practice
tasks.description: Implement a structured self-introduction action. (1) When meeting an NPC for the first time, the conversation system prompts: 'This is your first time meeting [NPC]. Introduce yourself!' (2) The NPC greets the player and waits for an introduction. (3) Introduction quality tracked: did the player give their name? Mention where they're from? State their purpose? Use appropriate greeting? Use formal/informal register correctly? (4) CEFR-scaled expectations: A1 = name only ('I am [name]'), A2 = name + origin, B1 = name + origin + purpose + polite phrases, B2 = full culturally appropriate introduction with small talk. (5) First introductions with NPCs are automatically quest objectives for early-game quests. (6) NPCs remember the introduction in future conversations. (7) Fire 'introduced_self' event for quest tracking.

tasks.title: Implement quest-driven vocabulary introduction before practice
tasks.description: Add a vocabulary pre-teaching step at the start of vocabulary-heavy quests. (1) When a quest is accepted that requires specific vocabulary, show a 'New Words' panel introducing 3-5 key words the player will need. (2) Each word shows: target language spelling, romanization, English meaning, example sentence, audio pronunciation (auto-played via TTS). (3) Player can tap each word to hear it again. (4) Brief flashcard review: show target language word, player guesses meaning, then reveal. (5) This ensures players aren't thrown into quests completely unprepared. (6) Vocabulary introduction words are added to the player's vocabulary list with 'new' mastery status. (7) The pre-teaching step is skippable for advanced players (B1+) or if the player already knows the words. (8) Link introduced vocabulary to the quest's targetWords so the spaced repetition system knows their origin.

tasks.title: Add NPC personality influence on quest dialogue style
tasks.description: Ensure NPC personalities (Big Five traits from context-manager.ts) meaningfully affect quest interactions. (1) High-extroversion NPCs are chatty and give verbose quest descriptions with extra context. (2) Low-agreeableness NPCs are brusque and require the player to be more formal/polite to complete conversation objectives. (3) High-openness NPCs accept creative language use and non-standard phrasing more readily. (4) High-conscientiousness NPCs require more precise language and correct grammar for quest completion. (5) High-neuroticism NPCs may give confusing directions that the player must interpret. (6) These personality effects are injected into the NPC's system prompt during quest-related conversations. (7) The quest feasibility validator accounts for NPC personality difficulty when setting quality thresholds. (8) This creates natural variety in quest experiences even for similar objective types.

tasks.title: Implement quest streak and bonus system
tasks.description: Add a streak bonus system for consecutive quest completions. (1) Track consecutive quest completions without failure or abandonment. (2) Streak multipliers: 2 in a row = 1.1x XP, 5 = 1.25x, 10 = 1.5x, 20 = 2.0x. (3) Display current streak in the quest log header with a flame icon. (4) Streak is maintained across game sessions (persisted in LanguageProgress). (5) Daily quests have their own separate streak counter. (6) Streak milestones trigger special achievements. (7) Losing a streak shows an encouraging message: 'Streak ended at [N]. Your best streak: [M]. Keep practicing!' (8) Mentor NPC acknowledges streaks: 'You've completed [N] quests in a row! Impressive!'

tasks.title: Implement conversation-only quest mode for purely verbal quests
tasks.description: Create a quest mode where all objectives are accomplished purely through conversation, with no physical game actions required. (1) These quests keep the player in conversation with an NPC for an extended dialogue (5-15 turns). (2) Objectives are tracked via conversation analysis only: use specific vocabulary, demonstrate grammar patterns, discuss specific topics, ask questions, answer questions. (3) The NPC guides the conversation through the objectives naturally. (4) Ideal for: players who prefer conversation over exploration, accessibility for players who struggle with navigation, focused language practice without distractions. (5) Conversation-only quests are tagged as such in the quest log. (6) Can be started from anywhere by approaching any quest-giving NPC. (7) Completion is detected when the conversation trigger analyzer confirms all verbal objectives are met.

tasks.title: Add error correction quests for common mistake remediation
tasks.description: Generate quests that specifically target the player's most common errors. (1) Analyze the player's grammar error history from LanguageProgress.grammarPatterns to find patterns with low accuracy. (2) Generate 'Correction Challenge' quests: NPC presents sentences with intentional errors (matching the player's common mistakes), player must identify and correct them. (3) 'Spot the Error' format: NPC says a sentence, player identifies what's wrong and provides the correction in target language. (4) Progressive: first identify the error type, then correct it, then use the correct form in a new sentence. (5) Track which error types improve after correction quests. (6) Limit to 1 error correction quest active at a time to avoid frustration. (7) These quests have gentler scoring (focus on improvement, not perfection). (8) NPC praises effort: 'You used to make this mistake often, but you're getting much better!'

tasks.title: Implement environmental interaction quests using world objects
tasks.description: Create quests that leverage the 3D world's environmental objects and buildings. (1) 'Building Inspector' quest: player must visit 5 buildings and describe each one (color, size, type) in target language. (2) 'Nature Walk' quest: player explores outdoor areas and names natural features (river, tree, mountain, sky, cloud) using examine_object. (3) 'Shop Window' quest: player reads shop signs (read_sign action) and reports back what each shop sells. (4) 'Town Map' quest: player creates a mental map by visiting landmarks and describing their relative positions. (5) Environmental quests use the procedurally generated buildings from ProceduralBuildingGenerator.ts, ensuring references match actual world geometry. (6) Building and object metadata includes target-language names generated during world creation. (7) These quests encourage exploration while building spatial and descriptive vocabulary.

tasks.title: Add haggle_price action for negotiation practice
tasks.description: Implement a price negotiation interaction for market/shop contexts. (1) When at a merchant NPC, player can initiate haggling by requesting a lower price in target language. (2) Merchant NPC responds based on personality (agreeableness affects willingness to negotiate). (3) Successful haggling requires: using number vocabulary correctly, using comparison phrases ('too expensive', 'how about X?'), polite but firm tone, providing reasons. (4) Haggling has rounds (max 3): player offers → merchant counters → player offers → resolution. (5) Vocabulary required: numbers, currency words, 'expensive', 'cheap', 'discount', 'deal', 'price'. (6) Grammar patterns: conditional ('If I buy two, can you...?'), comparative ('This is more expensive than...'). (7) Score based on: price achieved vs. starting price, language quality, cultural appropriateness. (8) Fire 'haggled' event for quest tracking.

tasks.title: Implement adaptive NPC speech complexity based on player level
tasks.description: Enhance context-manager.ts NPC system prompts to dynamically adjust speech complexity. (1) NPCs read the player's current CEFR level and adjust their language accordingly. (2) A1 players: NPC uses very simple sentences, repeats key words, mixes in more English, speaks slowly (TTS rate adjustment). (3) A2: NPC uses simple but varied sentences, less English mixing, gentle corrections. (4) B1: NPC uses natural speech with occasional complex structures, minimal English. (5) B2: NPC speaks naturally with idioms, humor, cultural references. (6) This applies to ALL NPC conversations, not just quest-related ones. (7) The adjustment is per-player, so the NPC adapts to whoever they're talking to. (8) If the player demonstrates higher comprehension than their CEFR level suggests, the NPC can tentatively increase complexity. (9) Quest NPCs are slightly simpler than ambient NPCs to reduce frustration during objectives.

tasks.title: Implement map-based quest navigation with target-language labels
tasks.description: Enhance the full-screen map view (Tab key) to support quest navigation with language learning. (1) Quest objective locations show as labeled markers on the map in the target language. (2) Street names display in the target language with English tooltip on hover. (3) Building names shown in target language. (4) Player can click a quest marker to set a navigation path. (5) Navigation path shows street names the player will traverse, acting as passive vocabulary exposure. (6) At A1-A2 level, map labels show both target language and English. At B1+, only target language (English on hover). (7) Integrate with the existing SettlementMiniMap.tsx and BabylonMinimap.ts. (8) Map becomes a study tool: player can review location names before navigating.

tasks.title: Add quest completion celebrations with language-specific flair
tasks.description: Enhance quest completion feedback with culturally-appropriate celebrations. (1) Quest completion triggers a celebration sequence scaled by quest difficulty: easy = small confetti + chime, medium = larger particle effect + fanfare, hard = full screen celebration with NPC congratulation. (2) NPC congratulation is in the target language: 'Félicitations!' / 'Excelente!' / etc. (3) Show a summary card: quest name, objectives completed, vocabulary learned, grammar practiced, XP earned, time taken. (4) For chain quest completion, show a special chain-complete animation. (5) For exam completion, show a grade card with dimension scores. (6) Celebration text includes a new target-language phrase the player learns as a bonus ('By the way, we say [phrase] when celebrating!'). (7) Sound effects appropriate to the world's cultural setting.

tasks.title: Implement quest difficulty auto-scaling based on performance
tasks.description: Add dynamic difficulty adjustment for quests based on player performance. (1) Track rolling average of quest completion quality (last 5 quests). (2) If average quality > 85%: increase difficulty slightly (add one more objective, increase vocabulary requirements, reduce hint availability). (3) If average quality < 50%: decrease difficulty (simplify objectives, pre-teach more vocabulary, increase NPC hint frequency). (4) Difficulty adjustments are subtle and don't change CEFR level (that's assessment-driven). (5) Track player frustration signals: abandoning quests, long pauses, repeated hint requests, low conversation quality. (6) Show difficulty level as a small icon in quest log (no explicit 'we made it easier' messaging to avoid discouragement). (7) Difficulty auto-scaling can be disabled in settings for players who prefer fixed difficulty. (8) Log all scaling decisions for research data.

tasks.title: Add sentence construction practice action
tasks.description: Add a 'sentence builder' mini-game that can be embedded in quests. (1) Present word tiles in the target language that the player must arrange into a correct sentence. (2) Words are displayed as draggable Babylon.js GUI elements. (3) Sentence prompts come from quest context: 'Make a sentence using [vocabulary word] and [grammar pattern]'. (4) Difficulty scales: A1 = 3-4 tiles, simple SVO order; A2 = 5-6 tiles with adjectives; B1 = 7-8 tiles with subordinate clauses; B2 = complex sentences with multiple clauses. (5) Timer optional. (6) Immediate visual feedback: correctly placed words snap into position with green glow, incorrect placement gets gentle shake. (7) Can be triggered as a quest objective mid-conversation: NPC asks 'Can you put these words in order?' (8) Fire 'sentence_built' event for quest tracking. (9) Track grammar accuracy for sentence construction separately.

tasks.title: Implement number and counting practice quests
tasks.description: Create quest templates specifically for number and counting vocabulary. (1) 'Inventory Count' quest: player counts items at various shops and reports numbers to an NPC in target language. (2) 'Market Prices' quest: player asks prices at shops and must remember/report them correctly. (3) 'Age Game' quest: player asks NPCs their age and reports the oldest/youngest to another NPC. (4) 'House Numbers' quest: player finds specific buildings by their address numbers, reading them in target language. (5) Number vocabulary is critical but often undertaught; these quests ensure systematic coverage: 1-10 at A1, 10-100 at A2, 100-1000 at B1, ordinals and fractions at B2. (6) Include time-telling exercises: 'Meet me at [time in target language]' quests where player must arrive at the correct time. (7) NPCs speak numbers at natural speed, requiring real-time comprehension.

tasks.title: Create onboarding quest sequence for new players
tasks.description: Design a structured onboarding quest chain that teaches both game mechanics and initial language. (1) Quest 0: 'Welcome' - mentor NPC initiates conversation, teaches player how to respond (teaches G key, R for speech, chat panel). (2) Quest 1: 'Look Around' - teaches examine_object action (X key) on 3 nearby objects, introduces first vocabulary words. (3) Quest 2: 'Take a Walk' - teaches movement to a nearby location, introduces direction words. (4) Quest 3: 'Make a Friend' - teaches NPC conversation by talking to a friendly NPC, introduces greetings. (5) Quest 4: 'Read the Signs' - teaches read_sign action, introduces environmental text. (6) Quest 5: 'First Assessment' - arrival assessment quest (from task 13). (7) Each quest has a pop-up tutorial explaining the relevant game controls. (8) Onboarding chain is mandatory for first play but skippable on replay. (9) Wire into OnboardingLauncher.ts existing system. (10) Quests auto-advance (no need to visit quest board).

tasks.title: Implement quest analytics and learning outcome tracking
tasks.description: Create server/services/quest-analytics.ts for tracking learning outcomes from quests. (1) After each quest completion, record: vocabulary words introduced, vocabulary words successfully used, grammar patterns practiced, grammar accuracy %, conversation turns, target-language ratio, time spent, hints used, attempts per objective. (2) Aggregate analytics per: quest type, objective type, vocabulary category, grammar pattern, CEFR level. (3) Expose via API: GET /api/worlds/:worldId/analytics/quests returns summary statistics. (4) Power the Learning Journal UI (task 51) with this data. (5) Track vocabulary retention: of words introduced in quests, what % are used correctly in subsequent quests? (6) Identify 'difficult' vocabulary/grammar for the player (low accuracy despite multiple encounters). (7) Feed analytics back into the quest recommendation engine. (8) All analytics stored efficiently in MongoDB with time-series bucketing.

tasks.title: Add phonetic similarity scoring for pronunciation objectives
tasks.description: Enhance pronunciation evaluation beyond string matching. (1) Create shared/language/phonetic-scorer.ts with phonetic similarity algorithms. (2) Convert STT transcription and target phrase to phonetic representation (IPA or simplified phonetic alphabet). (3) Score based on phonetic distance rather than exact spelling (e.g., 'bonjour' vs 'bonjur' should score highly since the pronunciation is close). (4) Language-specific phonetic rules: handle silent letters, liaisons, tonal markers, etc. (5) Partial credit for close phonemes (e.g., confusing 'b' and 'p' scores higher than confusing 'b' and 'z'). (6) Return specific feedback: 'You said [X sound] but the correct sound is [Y sound]. Try rounding your lips more.' (7) Use this scorer in pronunciation_check quest objectives instead of raw string comparison. (8) Build phonetic distance tables for the most commonly confused sound pairs per language.

tasks.title: Implement vocabulary category unlocking via quest progression
tasks.description: Create a progression system where quest completion unlocks new vocabulary categories for future quests. (1) Define vocabulary category tiers: Tier 1 (greetings, numbers, colors, family), Tier 2 (food, directions, weather, time), Tier 3 (professions, emotions, shopping, health), Tier 4 (politics, philosophy, abstract concepts, idioms). (2) Tier 1 is available from the start. (3) Complete 3+ quests using Tier 1 vocabulary → unlock Tier 2. Complete 5+ Tier 2 → unlock Tier 3. Complete 7+ Tier 3 → unlock Tier 4. (4) Unlocking shows a celebration: 'New vocabulary category unlocked: [category]!' (5) Quest generator only uses vocabulary from unlocked categories. (6) Track unlock progress in LanguageProgress. (7) Visual representation in quest log: vocabulary tree showing locked/unlocked categories. (8) Mentor NPC acknowledges new category unlocks with themed teaching.

tasks.title: Implement quest objective dependencies for ordered completion
tasks.description: Add dependency ordering between quest objectives so some must be completed before others. (1) Add 'dependsOn' field to quest objectives (array of objective IDs within the same quest). (2) Dependent objectives are grayed out in the checklist with 'Complete [prerequisite] first' label. (3) Example: in a delivery quest, 'collect_item' must complete before 'deliver_item'. In a learning quest, 'listen to NPC explain vocabulary' must complete before 'use vocabulary in conversation'. (4) QuestCompletionEngine respects dependencies: don't evaluate objectives whose prerequisites aren't met. (5) Circular dependency detection at quest creation time. (6) UI shows dependency arrows between objectives in the checklist (subtle connecting lines). (7) When a prerequisite completes, the dependent objective becomes active with a pulse animation. (8) Quest generator and templates can specify dependency chains.

tasks.title: Add collaborative NPC conversation quest (eavesdropping and joining)
tasks.description: Create quests where the player observes and then joins NPC-to-NPC conversations. (1) Leverage the existing AmbientConversationSystem.ts and NPCAmbientConversationManager.ts. (2) Quest objective: 'Listen to the conversation between [NPC A] and [NPC B] at [location]'. (3) Player approaches the conversing NPCs and sees their dialogue in speech bubbles (in target language). (4) After listening phase (30 seconds), quest prompts: 'Join the conversation. The NPCs are discussing [topic].'
