(5) Player enters the conversation and must contribute meaningfully to the topic being discussed. (6) Tests: listening comprehension (understanding what NPCs discussed), conversational joining (appropriate entry), topic maintenance (staying on subject). (7) This is realistic - in real immersion, learners often observe before participating. (8) NPC conversation topics are generated based on their occupations and relationship.

tasks.title: Implement weather and time-of-day vocabulary quests
tasks.description: Create quests that teach time and weather vocabulary using the game's dynamic systems. (1) 'Weather Report' quest: player observes current weather and describes it to an NPC in target language (sunny, rainy, cloudy, windy, hot, cold). (2) 'Time Check' quest: NPC asks player what time it is, player must respond in target language using the in-game clock. (3) 'Schedule Keeper' quest: player must meet NPCs at specific times, practicing time expressions ('at 3 o'clock', 'in the morning', 'before noon'). (4) 'Season Talk' quest: NPC discusses seasonal activities, player must respond with season-appropriate vocabulary. (5) Weather and time vocabulary are high-frequency practical words that enhance immersion. (6) Quest trigger analyzer detects correct time/weather vocabulary usage. (7) Visual cues in the game world change to match quest timing (dynamic skybox, lighting).

tasks.title: Add write_response action for letter and message composition
tasks.description: Implement written composition interactions. (1) NPC gives the player a writing task: write a letter, a notice, a shopping list, a diary entry. (2) Player uses the multi-line writing interface (from task 46) to compose their text. (3) Types of writing tasks: personal letter (informal register), official letter (formal register), notice board posting (informational), journal entry (reflective), recipe or instructions (imperative). (4) Each writing task has clear requirements: minimum word count, required vocabulary, required grammar structures. (5) NPC evaluates and provides markup: corrections in red, praise in green, suggestions in blue. (6) Written compositions are saved in the learning portfolio. (7) CEFR-scaled expectations: A1 = 2-3 sentences, A2 = short paragraph, B1 = structured paragraph, B2 = multi-paragraph with argumentation. (8) Fire 'composition_completed' event for quest tracking.

tasks.title: Implement quest board NPC with curated quest selection
tasks.description: Create a quest board NPC (town crier, bulletin board keeper) who presents available quests with context. (1) Quest board NPC is placed at a central location (town square) and always has the 'available quest' indicator. (2) When the player interacts, the NPC describes available quests conversationally in the target language: 'The baker needs help counting her deliveries' instead of a mechanical quest list. (3) Player can ask for more details about any quest. (4) NPC recommends quests based on the quest recommendation engine: 'I think you'd enjoy this one - it will help you practice [skill].' (5) Display a visual quest board (Babylon.js GUI) alongside the NPC showing 3-5 available quests. (6) Quest board refreshes periodically (every 10 quests completed or 15 minutes). (7) Wire into the existing notice board toggle (N key) when near the quest board NPC.

tasks.title: Implement quest progress persistence across game sessions
tasks.description: Ensure all quest progress is properly saved and restored. (1) On every objective progress update, persist to MongoDB via the existing PATCH /api/quests/:id endpoint. (2) When loading a game session, restore all active quests with their full objective state (completed flags, progress counters, hint states). (3) Restore quest chain progress and daily quest streaks. (4) Handle edge cases: quest references NPC who was removed, location that changed, items that were relocated. (5) Add a quest state migration system for when quest schemas change between versions. (6) Implement optimistic UI updates with eventual consistency (update UI immediately, sync to server in background). (7) Show a 'Saving...' indicator when quest state is being persisted. (8) Add a session resume notification: 'Welcome back! You have [N] active quests.'

tasks.title: Add peer comparison for quest performance (anonymized)
tasks.description: Show anonymized aggregate performance data for quest completion. (1) After completing a quest, optionally show: 'You scored [X]%. Average score for this quest: [Y]%.' (2) For vocabulary quests: 'You learned [N] words. Other learners averaged [M] words.' (3) Data is aggregated server-side from all players who completed the same quest template. (4) Comparison is encouraging, never discouraging: frame in terms of progress, not competition. (5) Only show comparisons when enough data exists (minimum 5 completions of the same template). (6) Can be toggled off in settings. (7) Store anonymized completion metrics in a separate collection: quest template ID, score, duration, CEFR level at time of completion. (8) This provides motivational context without creating pressure.
